runs.text: Native objects remain editable after delivery.
Spacing, roles, and containment remain deterministic.

slides.callout: Formatting integrity is a release gate, not a one-deck preference.

slides.title: One logical master, declared page archetypes
slides.subtitle: Page roles may differ without introducing accidental typography or spacing drift.

slides.title: Repeated components keep the same hierarchy

left.heading: HEADLINE
left.body: A stable component role controls type, emphasis, color, insets, and paragraph rhythm.

right.heading: BODY
right.body: Declared variants may change color while preserving the underlying hierarchy and spacing contract.

slides.title: Native tables keep symmetric cell margins
Icons explain the concepts they accompany

items.label: Goal
items.body: The outcome and why it matters.

slides.title: Dense copy moves to a roomier page

runs.text: Slidewright preserves the declared body role instead of shrinking below the minimum.
The continuation archetype keeps the same master, type system, and spacing scale.

slides.title: Repeated components remain identical across slides

left.heading: HEADLINE
left.body: A second page uses the same family, slot, variant, type, emphasis, insets, and paragraph rhythm.

right.heading: BODY
right.body: The exported deck now contains a genuine same-family and same-variant repetition for audit and visual review.
